title: Mpro-DockExec: a reporting-completeness and re-executability audit of the open-access SARS-CoV-2 Mpro docking literature
description: A 16-field reporting standard (MERS-Dock) and a five-level executability ladder (E0–E4) applied to 236 open-access SARS-CoV-2 main-protease (Mpro) molecular-docking papers, with re-execution of 37 quality-controlled ligand-level claims using AutoDock Vina 1.2.7. Only 8.1% of these open-access papers are directly re-executable (E3); none are fully reproducible with a disclosed seed and reusable configuration (E4; 0/236). Ships derived audit data (per-paper field states, executability labels), the reporting standard, and the analysis and re-execution scripts. Publisher full texts are not redistributed.
notes: Companion dataset and code for the bioRxiv preprint 'Most open-access Mpro docking papers are not directly re-executable' (forthcoming).
